exemplar.title: How does the lack of structure and communication in the main character's families of the Skins (2007) series affect their relationships and behavior outside the home, based on Minuchin's theory?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the lack of structure and communication in the main character's families of the Skins (2007) series affect their relationships and behavior outside the home, based on Minuchin's theory?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Research question clearly stated and maintained focus on three characters.
Title and abstract consistently signal the investigative purpose and theoretical lens.
Methodology outline allows partial replication and shows informed source selection.
weaknesses: Operational definitions of key terms (ârelationships and behaviourâ) remain broad.
Method description omits justification for episode selection criteria.
Connections between methodology and discussion are not uniformly sustained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in the upper 3â4 band. The research question and topic are communicated effectively and linked to discussion, but operational definitions and methodological detail are only partially realized. A score of 4 reflects this consistent competence without full topâband rigor.

strengths: Clear and coherent understanding of Minuchinâs theory, with correct use of specialist terms.
Relevant application of theory to character analyses, especially in the Cassie case study.
Integration of multiple source types to substantiate claims.
weaknesses: Lexical and grammatical errors occasionally obscure meaning.
Informal sources are used without evaluation of their reliability.
Bibliography contains incomplete or repeated entries.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a clear and coherent grasp of subject knowledge and generally appropriate application of source material, placing it at the upper end of the 3â4 band. Minor language imprecision and uneven source appraisal prevent a topâband score of 5â6, warranting a 4.

strengths: Adequate breadth of research with mixed scholarly and media sources largely pertinent to the question.
Logical linking of episode moments to theoretical constructs in analysis.
Coherent synthesis of caseâstudy findings in the discussion.
weaknesses: Limited critique of informal materialsâ credibility.
Analysis remains largely confirmatory, with few alternative interpretations.
Conclusions echo earlier points without fully weighing strengths and weaknesses.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall at the lower threshold of the 4â6 band: research is relevant but uncritical, analysis is logical but not deeply probing, and evaluation is coherent but superficial. A score of 4 accurately reflects this adequate but not yet substantial critical thinking.

strengths: All mandatory structural components (title page, contents, citations) are present.
Clear labelling of key subheadings supports reader navigation.
weaknesses: Table of contents omits page numbers, hindering navigation.
Inconsistent heading styles and truncated paragraphs disrupt flow.
Bibliography entries duplicated or incomplete and section breaks are misplaced.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Layout and structure are generally appropriate but executed inconsistently, with missing navigation aids and formatting irregularities. This aligns with the 1â2 band and justifies a score of 2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionâ"How does the lack of structure and communication in the main charactersâ families of the Skins (2007) series affect their relationships and behaviour outside the home, based on Minuchinâs theory?"âis clearly stated, appropriately focused on three named characters, and repeatedly referenced in section headings and analysis. The wording is precise enough to guide the investigation, though it remains somewhat broad (e.g. ârelationships and behaviourâ covers many variables) and could benefit from tighter operational definitions. Connection to the ensuing discussion is evident but not always sustained with equal depth. These qualities fit solidly within the 3-4 band, meriting a score of 4.

The student moves beyond description by linking specific episode moments to Minuchinâs constructs and comparing three case-study characters, which demonstrates **adequate but not yet penetrating analysis**. Reasoning is generally logical, yet it rarely interrogates alternative interpretations or limitations, and some assertions about cause-and-effect (e.g., parental behaviour directly producing adolescent pathology) are accepted without qualification. Occasional lapses into plot summary further limit depth. These characteristics place the work at the lower edge of the 4-6 band, meriting a score of 4.

Most cited episodes, theoretical texts and popular-media commentaries are relevant to the research question, and the student generally integrates quotations or scene references to substantiate claims. However, there is uneven critical appraisal: informal sources (e.g. blogs, CharacterTour, YouTube analyses) are used without evaluation of reliability, and some references are repeated or incomplete in the bibliography. These issues reduce overall appropriateness, placing the work in the 3-4 band at a score of 3.

Required layout components (title, word count, table of contents, in-text citations, bibliography) are present; nevertheless, **execution is inconsistent**. The table of contents omits page numbers, heading styles vary, bullet symbols are irregular, and some bibliography entries are duplicated or incomplete. Pagination within the sample is unclear. Such shortcomings indicate that layout elements are only partially correct, corresponding to the 1-2 band and justifying a score of 2.

The essay shows **clear and coherent understanding** of Minuchinâs structural family theory and applies key constructsâboundaries, hierarchy, subsystemsâto each character with reasonable accuracy. Specialist terminology such as âenmeshment,â âhierarchical inversion,â and âdiffuse boundariesâ is used correctly in most instances, indicating solid subject knowledge. Occasional misuse of words (e.g. âconveyance,â âdetentionâ) and minor conceptual imprecision prevent a move into the highest band, but the overall level corresponds to the upper end of the 3-4 range, justifying a score of 4.

The essay contains all mandatory sectionsâtitle page, abstract, contents, main body, bibliographyâand follows a logical sequence, making the structure **generally appropriate**. However, there are notable issues: duplicated or mis-numbered headings, an incomplete abstractâbody link, redundancies in section labelling, and occasional abrupt paragraph breaks that disrupt flow. These missing or flawed elements align with the 1-2 band, so a score of 2 is awarded.

The investigation draws on a mix of scholarly texts, popular commentaries, episode analyses and videos, producing **adequate breadth of research** that is largely relevant to the question. However, source credibility is taken on trust; there is limited critique of the informal materials, and some citations (e.g. real-estate website) appear tangential. The selection process is described only in outline. These factors align with the descriptors for the 4-6 band but at its lower threshold, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

The studentâs topicâexamining how dysfunctional family structures in Skins (2007) influence adolescent behaviour through the lens of Minuchinâs structural family theoryâis stated explicitly in the title, abstract and introduction. The focus on three principal characters is maintained throughout the sections, and the purpose of linking media representation with family-systems theory is consistently signposted. Occasional lapses in expression (e.g. awkward phrasing, minor grammatical errors) momentarily cloud clarity, but overall the topic and its relevance to English-language cultural studies are conveyed effectively. These features meet the descriptors for the 3-4 band and justify the upper score of 4 in this range.

The discussion section synthesises findings from the three character studies and relates them back to Minuchinâs theory, offering a coherent if **largely confirmatory** argument that dysfunctional structures impair adolescent adjustment. Yet, evaluation of evidence remains limited: counter-examples, alternative theoretical lenses, and methodological limitations are acknowledged only superficially. Conclusions mostly restate earlier points without fully weighing strengths and weaknesses. Consequently, the strand fits the 4-6 band but does not progress beyond its lower level, warranting a score of 4.

The student outlines the basic methodological stepsâviewing season 1, selecting three focal characters, identifying key episodes, and applying Minuchinâs framework. Citations of theoretical and secondary sources demonstrate some informed selection. Nevertheless, important procedural details remain implicit: criteria for episode/scene selection are not fully articulated, and no explanation is given for evaluating the credibility of on-line commentaries or YouTube videos. The description would allow partial replication but lacks the rigour and transparency expected for the top band. Consequently, the work meets the lower end of the 3-4 band, warranting a score of 3.

Key theoretical terms from Structural Family Therapy are regularly employed and generally accurate, supporting the analysis of character dynamics. Nevertheless, there are recurrent lexical and grammatical inaccuraciesâe.g. misusing âconveyance,â inconsistent tense, and awkward phrasingâthat at times obscure meaning. The terminology is more accurate than inaccurate, but it lacks the consistent precision demanded for the top band, positioning the work in the middle of the 3-4 band with a score of 3.

annotations.comment: The title precisely identifies both the media text and theoretical lens, establishing a clear and focused research direction that aligns effectively with the investigationâs objectives.
The subheading âImpact on His Relationships and Social Behaviorâ is consistently formatted, supporting clear progression in the character analysis.
Citation details are inconsistent (e.g. Minuchin 1999 vs. 1977); standardise the reference format (APA or MLA) for uniformity.
Claims about cause-and-effect between family structure and adolescent behaviour are asserted without acknowledging alternative explanations or limitations.
Typographical errors and awkward phrasing in the family categorisation paragraph reduce clarity; revise for precise language and correct terminology.
Including the word count at the front of the document meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to administrative detail.
The table of contents omits page numbers for each section, hindering navigation; add corresponding page numbers to improve usability.
The research question is clearly articulated and consistently framed around Minuchinâs theory and the focal characters, guiding the analysis with appropriate focus.
The bibliography heading appears immediately after the contents without a page break, disrupting the flow; separate it into its own section.
Occasional awkward phrasing and lexical choices in the introduction reduce clarity; proofread and refine vocabulary for more precise expression.
Informal analyses (e.g., blogs, YouTube) are integrated without discussing their reliability; evaluate each sourceâs appropriateness.
The analysis of Cassieâs interactions with Sid compellingly applies Minuchinâs theory to explain the impact of family dysfunction on her social behaviour.
The abstract summarises key findings and suggestions but omits mention of the methodological approach and sample selection criteria; include those details for completeness.
Include complete citation details (e.g. timestamps or page numbers) for Skybourn (2023) to strengthen academic rigour.
Avoid plot summary; deepen analysis by explicitly linking character actions to theoretical constructs with critical insight.
Theoretical sources are introduced without evaluating their credibility; include a brief critique of informal or non-peer-reviewed materials.
Subsection headings vary in style and numbering; apply a consistent hierarchy and formatting to all section titles.
The methodology description lacks justification for selecting specific episodes; define clear criteria to ensure replicability.
The subsection âApplication of Minuchinâs Conceptsâ uses inconsistent heading styling; ensure it matches other headings for cohesion.
The section on symptomatic patterns effectively connects theoretical terms to character behaviours, demonstrating solid analytical engagement.
The clear labelling of the Cassie Ainsworth case study enhances the structural flow and reader orientation.
Section 2.3 is clearly identified and maintains consistent formatting, aiding comprehension of the application of theoretical concepts.
The paragraph ends mid-discussion and appears truncated; complete the thought and verify all sentences conclude logically.
The discussion of parental roles and system dysfunction is well-structured, offering deeper analytical insights into boundary dynamics.
The description of Sidâs family environment uses confusing language; clarify references to avoid misinterpretation of character dynamics.
The link between paternal dynamics and Sidâs loyalty is asserted without empirical support; introduce literature evidence to substantiate this claim.
The bibliography includes irrelevant sources (e.g. a real-estate website) that do not contribute to the academic investigation; remove or replace with discipline-appropriate references.
